acceptance_criteria.description: Multi-action plans are bound to a fresh observation and lease; only the current boundary is authorized and stale continuation fails closed.
A material state change suspends an active plan and requires an explicit continue, replace, abort, or wait response; silence produces a no-op.
Dynamic step-locked and static event-gated profiles expose deterministic public decision boundaries with different inference cadence.

compatibility: Keep existing deterministic authority clocks and legacy fallback behavior unchanged.
migration: Only environments declaring a new decision profile use the interruptible runtime.
privacy: Decision events contain only observation-visible causes.
rollback: Disabling the new profile returns consumers to their prior execution loop.

summary: Execute agent plans in bounded deterministic leases so new observations can suspend stale intent and force explicit reconsideration.
title: Interruptible plans and decision-tempo runtime
